epic.description: Customer- and ops-facing web console for tracking parcels end to end: search, status overview, per-shipment detail with scan history and itinerary, and exception surfacing. Built on the shipment dataset (100 shipments, 640 scan events across 4 carriers).

features.description: Find and browse shipments quickly across the whole book of business.
Deep view of a single shipment: scan-event timeline, itinerary, and package attributes.
At-a-glance status counts and the ability to narrow the directory by status, carrier, and service level.
Make problem shipments and delivery progress impossible to miss.

stories.summary: Search shipments by tracking ID or keyword
stories.description: As an operations user, I want a single search box that matches on tracking ID, city, carrier, recipient, sender, and contents so that I can find any shipment without knowing which field it lives in.
acceptanceCriteria: Search matches across tracking ID, origin/destination/current city, carrier, sender, recipient, and contents category
Results update live as I type (no submit button)
Result count reflects the filtered set ("N of 100 shipments")
stories.businessValue: Cuts lookup time from minutes to seconds; the single most-used action for support agents fielding "where is my package" calls.
stories.techNotes: Client-side substring match over a precomputed haystack string per shipment; debounced input event.

stories.summary: Browse all shipments in a sortable directory
stories.description: As an ops user, I want a sortable table of all shipments so that I can scan the book of business and order it by the column I care about.
acceptanceCriteria: Table lists tracking ID, carrier, service, status, route, ETA, and weight
Clicking a column header sorts by it; clicking again reverses direction
Sort indicator (▲/▼) shows the active column and direction
stories.businessValue: Gives supervisors a single triage surface instead of exporting to spreadsheets.
stories.techNotes: Stable comparator keyed by column; status sorts by a fixed severity order rather than alphabetically.

stories.summary: See origin → destination route at a glance
stories.description: As an ops user, I want each row to show the origin and destination cities so that I can understand a shipment's lane without opening it.
acceptanceCriteria: Each row renders "Origin → Destination" with a clear arrow
Route is also a sortable column
stories.businessValue: Faster lane-level triage; supports spotting mis-routed shipments in the list.
stories.techNotes: Derived display field; route sort key is the concatenated origin+destination string.

stories.description: As an ops user, I want to open a shipment into a detail drawer so that I can see everything about it without leaving the directory.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a row opens a slide-in drawer with the shipment header, status, and progress bar
Drawer closes via the X button, clicking the overlay, or pressing Escape
Underlying list state (search/filter/sort) is preserved when the drawer closes
stories.businessValue: Keeps agents in flow; no full-page navigation means faster case handling.
stories.techNotes: Single reusable drawer element repopulated on open; overlay + transform transition; keydown listener for Escape.

stories.description: As a customer-facing agent, I want a timeline of scan events (most recent first) so that I can tell a customer exactly where their package has been.
acceptanceCriteria: Events render newest-first with description, facility, city/state, and timestamp
The most recent event is visually emphasized as the current status
Timeline handles shipments with 1 to 12 events without layout breakage
stories.businessValue: The core promise of a tracking product; directly reduces "where is it" contacts.
stories.techNotes: Reverse-ordered scanEvents array; CSS pseudo-element rail with a highlighted head node.

stories.summary: View planned itinerary with transport modes
stories.description: As an ops planner, I want to see the planned leg-by-leg itinerary with transport mode so that I can anticipate hand-offs and connection risk.
acceptanceCriteria: Each leg shows from → to, mode (air/truck/rail) with an icon, and planned departure/arrival
Legs render in planned order
stories.businessValue: Enables proactive intervention on at-risk connections before they become exceptions.
stories.techNotes: Maps itinerary[].mode to an icon lookup; planned times formatted to short dates.

stories.summary: See package dimensions, weight, and declared value
stories.description: As an ops user, I want package attributes (weight, dimensions, declared value, fragile/signature flags) so that I can handle claims and special-handling questions.
acceptanceCriteria: Detail shows weight, L×W×H dimensions, declared value, and piece count
Fragile, signature-required, and international flags render as tags when present
stories.businessValue: Supports claims, customs, and special-handling workflows from one screen.
stories.techNotes: Reads package.* and top-level international; tags rendered conditionally.

stories.summary: Status summary chips with live counts
stories.description: As a supervisor, I want status chips showing counts (delivered, in transit, exceptions, etc.) so that I can read the health of the book of business instantly.
acceptanceCriteria: Chips show a count per status plus an "All" total
Clicking a chip filters the directory to that status
The active chip is visually highlighted
stories.businessValue: Turns the console into a lightweight ops dashboard; surfaces spikes in exceptions.
stories.techNotes: Counts computed once from the dataset; chip click sets the status filter and re-renders.

stories.description: As an ops user, I want to filter the directory by a specific status so that I can work a single queue (e.g., only exceptions).
acceptanceCriteria: Selecting a status shows only shipments in that status
Status filter composes with search and other filters
Clearing the filter (All) restores the full list
stories.businessValue: Enables queue-based work assignment across a team.
stories.techNotes: Shared filter predicate; status filter is one clause AND-ed with the others.

stories.summary: Filter by carrier and service level
stories.description: As an ops user, I want carrier and service-level dropdowns so that I can focus on one carrier's shipments or expedited service tiers.
acceptanceCriteria: Carrier dropdown lists all carriers present in the data
Service dropdown lists levels in priority order (Overnight → Economy)
Both filters compose with search and status filter
stories.businessValue: Supports carrier-specific SLAs and expedited-shipment monitoring.
stories.techNotes: Options derived from the dataset; values AND-ed into the shared predicate.

stories.summary: Highlight exception shipments with an alert banner
stories.description: As a customer-facing agent, I want exception shipments to show a prominent alert with the reason so that I can proactively address problems.
acceptanceCriteria: Exception status renders in a distinct alert color in list and detail
Detail drawer shows an exception banner with the exception code and description
Banner only appears for shipments that actually have an exception
stories.businessValue: Drives proactive outreach on weather delays, address issues, and customs holds — the shipments most likely to generate complaints.
stories.techNotes: Reads exception.{code,description}; banner is conditional; status color tokens per status.

stories.description: As a customer-facing agent, I want a progress bar and clear ETA on each shipment so that I can set accurate expectations.
acceptanceCriteria: Detail drawer shows a progress bar mapped to the shipment's status
Estimated delivery and delivered/shipped timestamps are shown and human-readable
Delivered shipments show the actual delivery time
stories.businessValue: Improves first-contact resolution by giving agents an ETA they can quote with confidence.
stories.techNotes: Status→progress mapping; date formatting via toLocaleString; delivered vs shipped chosen by presence of deliveredAt.
